caption: Closes #996: a queued waypoint leg now applies its own recorded stance once the route promotes it, instead of silently inheriting whatever stance was already active. The far (north) regiment is put on Hold immediately and never moves. The near (south) regiment's first leg marches under Normal (no stance armed); a second leg is then queued under Hold (arming a stance before a Shift+right-click waypoint append stamps that stance onto just the newly queued leg). Once the first leg finishes and the queue promotes the second, the near regiment's stance switches to Hold and it correctly stays put at its new position instead of auto-advancing into the far regiment, 130 world units away -- before this fix, the appended leg's stance was captured but never applied, so the promoted leg would have silently kept the first leg's Normal stance, and the regiment would have marched into a fight neither side was ordered to start.
